key_insight: The paper extends compatible-strain mixed finite elements from simplicial meshes to general quadrilateral meshes for 2D nonlinear elasticity, preserving the differential structure needed for stable compressible and incompressible formulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables more flexible and efficient quadrilateral discretizations for nonlinear solid mechanics, with fewer degrees of freedom than comparable second-order simplicial elements.
method: Compute shape functions directly in physical space via numerical integration, avoiding Piola-map limitations on general quadrilaterals.
Use first-kind Nedelec elements to interpolate the displacement gradient and introduce compatible stress shape functions.
Formulate both compressible and incompressible versions, with element-level pressure condensation for the incompressible case.
evidence_signal: Numerical examples show the quadrilateral elements can solve problems that previously required second-order simplicial compatible-strain elements while using fewer degrees of freedom.
watch_item: The construction relies on direct physical-space integration because standard Piola transformations are only compatible for special quadrilateral geometries, which may increase implementation complexity.
paper_title: First-Order Compatible-Strain Mixed Quadrilateral Finite Elements for 2D Nonlinear Elasticity
